children.heading: Loans and grants to persons other than individuals
children.label: a Loans and grants to persons other than individuals
i
children.text: the per capita income of the residents of which is not more than 70 percent of the national average per capita income, as determined by the Department of Commerce; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the unemployment rate of the residents of which is not less than 125 percent of the national average unemployment rate, as determined by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), loans and grants under paragraph (1) may also be made if the loan or grant funds will be used primarily to provide water or waste services, or both, to residents of a rural area that was recognized as a colonia as of .October 1, 1989

children.heading: Loans and grants to individuals
children.label: b Loans and grants to individuals

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make or insure loans and make grants to individuals who reside in a community described in subsection (a)(1) for the purpose of extending water supply and waste disposal systems, connecting the systems to the residences of the individuals, or installing plumbing and fixtures within the residences of the individuals to facilitate the use of the water supply and waste disposal systems. Such loans shall be at a rate of interest no greater than the Federal Financing Bank rate on loans of a similar term at the time such loans are made. The repayment of such loans shall be amortized over the expected life of the water supply or waste disposal system to which the residence of the borrower will be connected.

children.heading: Manner in which loans and grants are to be made
children.label: 2 Manner in which loans and grants are to be made
A
children.text: directly to such individuals by the Secretary; or

children.label: B
children.text: to such individuals through the rural water supply corporation, cooperative, or similar entity, or public agency, providing such water supply or waste disposal services, pursuant to regulations issued by the Secretary.

under subsection (a) to rural water supply corporations, cooperatives, or similar entities, or public agencies, that propose to provide water supply or waste disposal services to the residents of those rural subdivisions commonly referred to as colonias, that are characterized by substandard housing, inadequate roads and drainage, and a lack of adequate water or waste facilities; and

under subsection (b) to individuals who reside in a rural subdivision commonly referred to as a colonia, that is characterized by substandard housing, inadequate roads and drainage, and a lack of adequate water or waste facilities.

children.heading: “Cooperative” defined
children.label: d “Cooperative” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “cooperative” means a cooperative formed specifically for the purpose of the installation, expansion, improvement, or operation of water supply or waste disposal facilities or systems.

children.label: A
children.text: for grants under this section, $30,000,000 for each fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: for loans under this section, $30,000,000 for each fiscal year; and

children.label: C
children.text: in addition to grants provided under subparagraph (A), for grants under this section to benefit Indian tribes (as defined in ), $20,000,000 for each fiscal year.section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: An entity eligible to receive funding through a grant made under shall not be eligible for a grant from funds made available under paragraph (1)(C).section 1926d of this title

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Secretary shall issue interim final regulations, with a request for public comments, implementing this section.October 28, 1992
